what_you_do: I help burned-out corporate marketers replace their salary with a freelance client base so they can quit the 9-to-5 without gambling their mortgage.
pricing: Done a few at $1500. Want to sell a $3k, 90-day program.
best_client: Sarah, 38, marketing manager at a SaaS company. Great at her craft, terrified to go out on her own. Within 90 days she landed three retainer clients at $2k/mo each and gave notice. What she kept saying before we started: "I know I'm good at this, I just don't know how to get people to pay me for it without feeling gross." 90 days in, clients like her have 2-4 retainers and a repeatable way to get more.
process: First we pick one niche and one offer. Then we build a one-page pitch and a warm-outreach list of 50. Then we run daily outreach with scripts until the first client says yes, and systematize from there.
story: I got laid off in 2019 with a kid on the way and no plan. I white-knuckled my way to my first $5k month, then reverse-engineered what actually worked. What makes me different: 12 years inside marketing departments, so I teach them to sell the exact way corporate buyers actually buy — not the guru DM-spam playbook.
objections: Before buying people say: "I'm not ready," "What if I can't get clients?", "Is this just another course?", "I don't have time while working full-time." What they've tried that failed: cold DM courses, a $2k mastermind that was all mindset and no system, and posting on LinkedIn hoping clients would appear. The real thing stopping them is no repeatable way to find clients — it's all referrals and luck.
